selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = null

response_raw:
{
"mode": "llm",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to adopt a multi-faceted approach that involves both technological and human interventions. Firstly, social media platforms must invest in advanced AI algorithms that can detect and flag fake content in real-time. This will help to reduce the spread of misinformation and increase user trust in these platforms. Secondly, social media companies should provide users with easy-to-use tools that allow them to verify the authenticity of content before sharing it. For instance, fact-checking features can be integrated into the content sharing process, enabling users to quickly assess the credibility of a piece of information. Thirdly, social media platforms must work towards creating a culture of transparency and accountability, where users are encouraged to report fake content and hold each other accountable for spreading false information. By fostering a sense of community responsibility, social media can become safer and more reliable spaces for users.",
"reasoning_summary": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to adopt a multi-faceted approach that involves both technological and human interventions. Firstly, social media platforms must invest in advanced AI algorithms that can detect and flag fake content in real-time. Secondly, social media companies should provide users with easy-to-use tools that allow them to verify the authenticity of content before sharing it. Thirdly, social media platforms must work towards creating a culture of transparency and accountability, where users are encouraged to report fake content and hold each other accountable for spreading false information.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to adopt a multi-faceted approach that involves both technological and human interventions. Firstly, social media platforms must invest in advanced AI algorithms that can detect and flag fake content in real-time. This will help to reduce the spread of misinformation and increase user trust in these platforms. Secondly, social media companies should provide users with easy-to-use tools that allow them to verify the authenticity of content before sharing it. For instance, fact-checking features can be integrated into the content sharing process, enabling users to quickly assess the credibility of a piece of information. Thirdly, social media platforms must work towards creating a culture of transparency and accountability, where users are encouraged to report fake content and hold each other accountable for spreading false information. By fostering a sense of community responsibility, social media can become safer and more reliable spaces for users.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to adopt a multi-faceted approach that involves both technological and human interventions. Firstly, social media platforms must invest in advanced AI algorithms that can detect and flag fake content in real-time. Secondly, social media companies should provide users with easy-to-use tools that allow them to verify the authenticity of content before sharing it. Thirdly, social media platforms must work towards creating a culture of transparency and accountability, where users are encouraged to report fake content and hold each other accountable for spreading false information.
